title: Aspect Ratio Calculator
description: Calculate aspect ratios, find missing dimensions, and resize while keeping proportions. Perfect for video and web.

results.empty: Enter dimensions to calculate the aspect ratio

howToUse.label: Enter dimensions
howToUse.description: Type a width and height in pixels (or any unit). The aspect ratio is calculated instantly.

howToUse.label: Find a missing dimension
howToUse.description: Switch to the second mode, enter the ratio and one dimension to calculate the other proportionally.

howToUse.label: Compare to common ratios
howToUse.description: The tool shows the nearest common aspect ratios (16:9, 4:3, 1:1, etc.) for reference.

faq.question: What is an aspect ratio?
faq.answer: An aspect ratio describes the proportional relationship between an image's width and height. It is written as W:H (e.g., 16:9) or as a decimal (e.g., 1.78). The same aspect ratio can apply to any resolution, from 1280×720 to 3840×2160 — both are 16:9.

faq.question: What are common aspect ratios?
faq.answer: 16:9 — widescreen HD/4K video and monitors. 4:3 — older TVs and displays. 1:1 — square (Instagram). 21:9 — ultrawide monitors. 9:16 — vertical video (Reels, TikTok). 3:2 — DSLR / mirrorless camera sensors.

faq.question: How do I resize an image while keeping the aspect ratio?
faq.answer: Enter your original dimensions to find the ratio, then switch to "From ratio + one side" mode and enter the new width or height. The tool calculates the other dimension automatically.

faq.question: What is the golden ratio?
faq.answer: The golden ratio is approximately 1.618:1 (or 1:0.618). It is considered aesthetically pleasing and appears frequently in art, architecture, and nature. The ratio is φ (phi) ≈ 1.6180339887.
